description_long: This is a small, single-story house with a simple rectangular footprint and a prominent gable roof covered in rust-colored metal sheets. The front facade features a covered porch supported by two square columns, a main entry door with a metal security grille, and a single window also protected by a grille. The exterior walls are finished in two-tone plaster, with a lighter upper section and a darker base, and the house sits elevated on a concrete platform with two steps leading up to the porch. The side and rear walls are plain, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The roof overhangs the structure, providing some shelter to the entryway and porch area. The overall appearance suggests a modest, functional urban dwelling, likely found in tropical or subtropical regions.
description: A modest, single-story house with a rusted gable roof, covered porch, and barred entry door and window, set on a raised concrete platform. The exterior is finished in two-tone plaster and features a simple, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entry door with metal grille, barred window, and two steps leading up to the house.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain gable end wall, the overhanging roof, and the raised concrete platform.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a featureless wall with two-tone plaster and the full span of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the plain wall and roof overhang.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with two steps, square columns, and a metal-grilled entry door
